item.title: Marvin Gaye
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul, psychedelic soul, quiet storm Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1961–1984 (primary period: 1964–1973) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Listen to the shift between “I Heard It Through the Grapevine” (1968) and What’s Going On (1971): from a single built on paranoia and rhythmic ...

item.title: Burt Bacharach
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, traditional pop, film score Scene: Brill Building Era: 1957–2023 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Burt Bacharach is the most harmonically sophisticated songwriter in the history of American pop — the figure who brought Ravel, Debussy, and Brazilian bossa nova into the three-minute singl...

item.title: The Rolling Stones
item.description: Genres: British blues, blues rock, rock & roll, roots rock Scene: London (Richmond, then Soho) Era: 1962–present (primary period: 1968–1972) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Keith Richards plays behind the beat.

item.title: The Who
item.description: Genres: Mod, freakbeat, art rock, hard rock, rock opera Scene: Mod, Shepherd’s Bush, London Era: 1964–present (primary period: 1965–1973) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Most drummers keep time.

item.title: Blues
item.description: Period: c. 1900–present Geographic origin: African American communities of the U.S.

item.title: Country blues
item.description: Blues | American folk music → Country blues Period: c. 1920s–1940s (recorded era); roots c.

item.title: Delta blues
item.description: Blues → Country blues → Delta blues Period: c. 1920s–1940s (recorded era); roots c.

item.title: British blues
item.description: Blues → Electric blues → British blues Period: c.

item.title: Chicago blues
item.description: Blues → Electric blues → Chicago blues Period: Late 1940s–present (peak: 1948–mid-1960s) Location: Chicago, Illinois (South Side and West Side) Key influences: Delta blues, country blues, jump blues RYM Muddy Waters’s slide guitar cutting through the noise of a South Side club at two in the morning,...

item.title: Jump blues
item.description: Blues → Jump blues Period: Late 1930s–mid-1950s Location: Kansas City, Los Angeles, New York, Chicago Key influences: Swing, big band, boogie woogie, blues RYM A saxophone honks a two-bar riff, a piano hammers boogie woogie patterns underneath, and a singer half-shouts a lyric about drinking, dancin...
